steps.title: Find the defining contrast
steps.content: The passage sets up two approaches with advantages, then uses “But” to reveal a key limitation: when results contradict, it exposes that each approach can fail to account for relevant factors affecting the animals' behavior.

steps.title: Select the answer matching this logic
steps.content: Choice C describes both approaches as viable (each can be effectively studied) while acknowledging that both have drawbacks that could affect the accuracy of the findings — exactly mirroring the passage's structure of advantages followed by a crucial “But” exposing limitations. Choices A, B, and D either rank one method as superior, claim conflicts are inevitable, or overstate that both studies definitely had flaws rather than the passage's more cautious one or both. That matches choice C — C is correct.
